title: Buying 111222 etc or waiting for a complete tetralogy
display_title: Buying 111222 etc or waiting for a complete tetralogy
wikitext:
[]: Buying 1.11,2.22 etc or waiting for a complete tetralogy?             Quote Post by transcendent12 » Tue May 08, 2012 4:33 pm Hi guys, as a newcomer to the series (and anime in general^^) I was wondering peoples opinions on individually getting the blu ray releases like the nice orange and red ones from Japan for 1.11 2.22 etc. OR waiting until there's some complete tetralogy. I'd want the "best" option in terms of having a collectable item. At the moment, the Japanese releases arn't too difficult to get hold of and not toooo expensive. But I imagine a few years down the line it might be more difficult? So basically, definitive tetralogy collection (speculative) vs getting the best editions of the movies of rebuild individually? Thanks in advance ^^

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue May 08, 2012 5:08 pm I think the most collectible item are the Japanese LE releases that come with film strips. I think there may be other countries that also do film strips in their releases but Funimation hasn't. Those Japanese LE tend to go relatively quickly, so you may not be able to buy one new a couple of years after the fact. As far as the Funimation releases, it probably doesn't matter either way, assuming they do a boxset release.

[]:             Quote Post by transcendent12 » Tue May 08, 2012 5:28 pm ah yes, getting the first edition releases would be a bit too much effort. Being first to news of releases is hard enough when it starts in Japanese. I'll probably just aim to get a matching set of the 4 movies. Just not the UK ones, they're really boring with the standard blu ray strip in the top of the case :P

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Tue May 08, 2012 9:09 pm I'm going to go for the Tetralogy and see what I can do about seeing the movies online.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue May 08, 2012 10:17 pm It's currently not that difficult to get a used copy of the Japanese LEs, but keep in mind that many of the more "desirable" film strips (like ones with close-ups of main characters) already have been sold by themselves for a premium on the secondary market by their original buyers. So if you do get a used LE w/ filmstrip, the odds of getting a really "good" one are reduced. I got lucky and at least got something visually interesting, even if it isn't an Eva or main character... SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by transcendent12 » Wed May 09, 2012 1:09 am Also, just to confirm, none of the Japanese blu-ray releases have english subs do they? Only funimation/UKmanga/hongkong do. Which further breaks down into: Funimation - 1.11 cover is a low shot of and EVA, 2.22 is orange sleeve but Mari on the jewel case cover? UK - standard pictures, standard cases Japan - 1.11 silver/red case. 2.22 orange case of similar construction as 1.11. Hong Kong - has a inferior quality version of the Japanese themed release but has english subs / wrong region It's a shame, cause I really like the Japanese release but I don't think I can go so far as to get them on top of the funimation / UK / mixing them together :P (I try to maintain some level of practicality :P) I expect the funimation/UK versions will always be easy to get for some time (And cheap) so i'll always be able to get a good copy for actually WATCHING evangelion. But I think I'll hold hope for some Tetralogy super slick release which has english subs and looks great as well.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed May 09, 2012 3:12 am I'm just going to buy it again if they release it in a collection.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu May 10, 2012 3:22 am transcendent12 wrote: Also, just to confirm, none of the Japanese blu-ray releases have english subs do they? Only funimation/UKmanga/hongkong do. Which further breaks down into: Funimation - 1.11 cover is a low shot of and EVA, 2.22 is orange sleeve but Mari on the jewel case cover? UK - standard pictures, standard cases Japan - 1.11 silver/red case. 2.22 orange case of similar construction as 1.11. Hong Kong - has a inferior quality version of the Japanese themed release but has english subs / wrong region It's a shame, cause I really like the Japanese release but I don't think I can go so far as to get them on top of the funimation / UK / mixing them together :P (I try to maintain some level of practicality :P) I expect the funimation/UK versions will always be easy to get for some time (And cheap) so i'll always be able to get a good copy for actually WATCHING evangelion. But I think I'll hold hope for some Tetralogy super slick release which has english subs and looks great as well.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat May 12, 2012 11:57 am You live in America, you might as well stick with the Funimation releases. Living in the UK... well, since Funi makes their Blu's A and B, decide for yourself, if it's worth the price. Honestly, for me, it's not really worth importing the original Blu-rays since Japan always charges their content at a high price. (I'd buy 7 $10 Blu-rays at a price of Japan's EVA 2.22 Blu-ray, as shown on CD Japan. Ridiculous [from an American's point of view, of course.]) Plus, a film strip based upon a film that was animated, edited, and transferred digitally? ... yeah, a USB drive with a Raw digital version of a scene might be worth more than a Film strip in this case. As for Hong Kong: STAY AWAY FROM RELEASES MADE IN THIS COUNTRY. Not only will you be ripped off, but the quality sucks (at times) compared to what Japan and America present.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sat May 12, 2012 12:14 pm tomrule123 wrote: As for Hong Kong: STAY AWAY FROM RELEASES MADE IN THIS COUNTRY. Not only will you be ripped off, but the quality sucks (at times) compared to what Japan and America present.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat May 12, 2012 1:05 pm svenge wrote: On a tangent, I'm getting the HK BD release of Laputa (released by long-time Studio Ghibli licensee IVL) because the upcoming Disney US release has dub-titles instead of literal subtitles .

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sat May 12, 2012 8:47 pm tomrule123 wrote: Whoa whoa whoa... only dub-titles? You're joking, right? That's impossible. They're supposed to include two subs, one for Dub (SDH) and translated subs, like they do for the DVD versions. Even Ponyo and Nausicaa Blu-ray had both dub and sub-titles. That does it! Not buyin'. Thankfully, I know another way to see this in HD... and I won't tell anyone. (reads forums on Blu-ray.com) oh hey, I didn't know you're on the forums of Blu-ray.com!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat May 12, 2012 10:06 pm If you're going to wait for a complete tetralogy, you're going to be waiting until at least early 2015. Final is to come out 2013, it will take a good year for it to come out over here, and then they won't release a full set right away, so realistically, 2015 is the earliest they would put out a combo release.

[]:             Quote Post by transcendent12 » Sun May 13, 2012 9:50 pm tomrule123 wrote: You live in America, you might as well stick with the Funimation releases. Living in the UK... well, since Funi makes their Blu's A and B, decide for yourself, if it's worth the price. Honestly, for me, it's not really worth importing the original Blu-rays since Japan always charges their content at a high price. (I'd buy 7 $10 Blu-rays at a price of Japan's EVA 2.22 Blu-ray, as shown on CD Japan. Ridiculous [from an American's point of view, of course.]) Plus, a film strip based upon a film that was animated, edited, and transferred digitally? ... yeah, a USB drive with a Raw digital version of a scene might be worth more than a Film strip in this case. As for Hong Kong: STAY AWAY FROM RELEASES MADE IN THIS COUNTRY. Not only will you be ripped off, but the quality sucks (at times) compared to what Japan and America present.

[]:             Quote Post by Redaye » Mon May 14, 2012 3:46 pm Waiting for the completed tetra. Laziness I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Jul 17, 2012 12:43 am Will probably wait for a tetralogy SE release if that comes out since I usually avoid single releases. Also by the time an SE release drops they tend to add more features and bonuses to it. The last time I bought multiple versions of something was for Battle Royale I and II and I'm past that phase. That film cell for 2.22 is mighty tempting though. Here's a thought: What if there were to be 4 film strips included in a LE tetralogy release?
